medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Born small for gestational age, positive Guthrie test, blood phenylalanine as high as 2210 pmol/l, reduced to 30 pmol/l with low-phenylalanine diet, occasional refusal of feeding, slightly retarded psychomotor development
medical_history.past_medical_history: Hypotrophic newborn, not breast fed, high protein milk formula
medical_history.family_history: Mother and maternal grandfather showed retarded hyperphenylalaninaemia after loading with phenylalanine

physical_examination.general_examination: Weight and length below the 3rd percentile, Apgar score at 1 min was 10

laboratory_examinations.findings: Blood phenylalanine was as high as 2210 μmol/l
laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: Reduced blood phenylalanine to 30 μmol/l within 1 week with low-phenylalanine diet
Decreased to 2.3 mmol/mol creatinine at age 5 months

laboratory_examinations.findings: Plasma phenylalanine decreased from 402 to 182, 108, 95, and 123 μmol/l within 2, 4, 6, and 12h, respectively
Plasma phenylalanine concentrations after 6h of 1590, 772, 508, 250, and 318 μmol/l in patient, mother, maternal grandfather, father, and maternal grandmother, respectively
Neopterin 25 nmol/l, biopterin 34 nmol/l, homovanillic acid 358 nmol/l, 5-hydroxyindole acetic acid 262 nmol/l, phenylalanine 18 μmol/l, tyrosine 12 μmol/l
Neopterin 5.53 mmol/mol creatinine, biopterin 0.64 mmol/mol creatinine

other_tests.findings: Patient F.S.: 4.0 pU/g Hb (23%), Mother F.D.: 3.9 pU/g Hb (22%), Father V.N.: 17.1 pU/g Hb (97%)

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Peripheral tetrahydrobiopterin deficiency with hyperphenylalaninaemia due to incomplete 6-pyruvoyl tetrahydropterin synthase deficiency or heterozygosity
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on biochemical characterization including tetrahydrobiopterin-responsive hyperphenylalaninaemia, a high neopterin/biopterin ratio in urine and plasma, and normal or elevated concentrations of biopterin, homovanillic acid, and 5-hydroxyindole acetic acid in cerebrospinal fluid. Measurements of the activity of erythrocyte 6-pyruvoyl tetrahydropterin synthase (PTS) and phenylalanine loading tests in the patients and their parents supported the diagnosis.
